_comment: Shared roommate/household config — the single source of truth for WHO is commuting, with TWO scrape profiles. 'liam' = solo: always the HackerRank office; that scraper looks for rooms/studios/1bd everywhere on the corridor. 'group' = the shared-household profile; that scraper targets ~N bedrooms (1:1 people->bedrooms; targetBedrooms overrides) everywhere. BOTH target the same broad geography (searches.json covers South Bay + Peninsula + SF; apartment searches run up to ~$6000 so group-sized homes are captured). The dashboard seeds its Liam/Group profiles from here (export_housing_data.py -> defaultLiam + defaultPeople) and its '⧉ Copy as scrape config' button writes the active profile back here. Per-person: arrival (HH:MM, next Mon/Wed/Thu), car/bike (route-optimization options).
